text: Using HTTPS is a fundamental step in web security because it encrypts data between the user’s browser and the server. This prevents attackers from intercepting sensitive information like passwords or credit card numbers. Equally important is authentication, which ensures that only authorized users can access certain parts of a website. Strong passwords and multi-factor login methods can greatly reduce risks. Developers must also practice careful input validation to guard against common vulnerabilities, such as SQL injection or cross-site scripting. By checking and cleaning all user input, websites become much harder to exploit. Taken together, these measures help protect both users and the integrity of the web application.
